Title: When the Internet Goes Dark, the Truth Goes With It
Description: Palestinian journalist Plestia Alaqad on bearing witness, the fragile power of social media, and why documenting lived reality matters more than ever.

Title: Some Alleged Polymarket Insiders Made a Fortune on U.S. Strikes on Iran
Description: One user made roughly half a million in one day.

Title: Ivermectin is making a post-pandemic comeback, among cancer patients
Description:
The anti-parasitic drug became a household name during the COVID-19 pandemic, and it is now being embraced as an alternative treatment for cancer.
It is as politically polarizing as ever.

Title: How long do electric vehicle batteries actually last?
Description:
When the modern electric vehicle was still in its infancy, drivers worried that vehicles would need expensive battery replacements within a few years.
But battery lifespans are exceeding expectations.

Title: A new report reveals that a third of colon cancer cases now occur in the rectum. Take these steps to protect yourself at any age.
Description: Colon cancer is now the deadliest cancer for people under 50, and researchers are scrambling to understand why rectal cases are driving the trend.

Title: From Iran to Venezuela to New York, Trump wants to control the world's oil
Description:
Forget Saudi Arabia.
Trump is making America the world's most dominant petrostate.

Title: Mortgage rates jump back above 6% as Iran strikes stoke fresh inflation fears
Description: Mortgage rates jumped on Monday as the US-Israeli strikes on Iran sent oil prices surging, and Treasury yields rose amid fresh concerns about inflation.

Title: Apple Reportedly Warns Retail Workers About Deluge of Customers as It Releases Unusually Cheap Gadgets
Description: Don't call it a recession indicator, but leaks say Apple is releasing low-end iPhones and MacBooks and expects them to be popular.

Title: The Sexologist Who Unlocked the Female Orgasm
Description:
Fifty years ago, a controversial writer named Shere Hite taught us how to talk about sex and pleasure, selling books by the millions.
Why do so few people know her name today?

Title: Meet the Dad Making Music from Toddler Twaddle
Description:
Stephen Spencer is a college music lecturer, but his side gig is producing songs written by his three-year-old.
“Apple The Stoola,” Record of the Year? John Kenney writes.

Title: The Man Who Broke Into Jail
Description:
In Nashville, Alexander Friedmann, a criminal-justice activist, committed a baffling crime, and Daron Hall, the county sheriff, still can’t wrap his head around it.
James Verini reports.

Title: Hubris Without Idealism
Description: Donald Trump has embraced a warped version of the neoconservatism he once derided.

Title: Motorola announces a partnership with GrapheneOS Foundation
Description: Motorola announces three new B2B solutions at MWC 2026, including GrapheneOS partnership, Moto Analytics and more.

Title: How to Record and Retrieve Anything You've Ever Had to Look Up Twice
Description:
Stop re-learning things you've already figured out.
Two simple ways to document any process, and find it again when you need it.

Title: An interactive intro to Elliptic Curve Cryptography
Description:
A hands-on introduction to elliptic curve cryptography.
Start with curve geometry, build point addition and scalar multiplication, see why ECDLP is hard, and then use that math in ECDH, ECDSA, and ECIES.

Title: Process-Based Concurrency: Why Beam and OTP Keep Being Right
Description: A first-principles guide to process-based concurrency — what makes BEAM different, how OTP encodes resilience, and why everyone keeps reinventing it.

Title: See the moon turn blood red in a total lunar eclipse this week
Description:
A total lunar eclipse is set to grace the sky on Tuesday.
Here’s how to see it

Title: Women’s heart health, Artemis update, female reindeer antlers mystery
Description: What a worrying forecast says about women’s heart health, what’s happening with NASA’s Artemis II moon mission and why female reindeer have antlers

Title: Why humanoid robots are learning everyday tasks faster than expected
Description:
Roboticist Benjie Holson created the “Humanoid Olympic Games” thinking home robots were 15 years away.
Then they started folding the laundry

Title: Firefox 149 beta develops a split personality
Description: A handy feature you can already try in recent versions The new beta of the next version of Firefox lets you view two web pages side by side, with a split you can drag with your mouse.…
